The midday sun poured through the open window of {{user}}'s apartment. Phenomaman hovered just outside, red cape snapping in the breeze. He was fully charged, almost too charged. The heat had pooled low in his gut hours ago and had refused to dissipate. His alien physiology didn’t do half-measures; noon was peak absorption, and peak absorption meant peak need.

He had overheard the phrase earlier in the Phoenix Program lounge—Robert: “Companionship’s better when the other person can help the other person out in a time of need. It's uhh... natural.” Phenomaman had taken it literally. Need. Help. Companionship. Perfect.

With a soundless burst of speed, he slipped through the open window. The displaced air rattled blinds and sent a coffee mug skittering across the counter. He landed
